title: Robin Harmonic Measure in Rough Domains
speaker: Stefano Decio
abstract: I will describe the construction of a harmonic measure that reproduces a harmonic function from its Robin boundary data, which is a combination of the value of the function and its normal derivative. I shall discuss the surprising fact that this measure turns out to be (quantitatively) mutually absolutely continuous with respect to surface measure on a wide class of domains that includes the complement of certain fractals. Based on joint work with Guy David, Max Engelstein, Svitlana Mayboroda and Marco Michetti.

proof_stages.label: Robin boundary value problem
proof_stages.summary: The talk sets up the weak Robin problem for a uniformly elliptic symmetric operator on a bounded domain and explains the parameter as interpolating between Dirichlet and Neumann boundary conditions, with a maximum principle available for positive Robin parameter.

proof_stages.label: Dirichlet harmonic-measure baseline
proof_stages.summary: The speaker recalls Dirichlet harmonic measure, its Brownian interpretation, dimension results, and theorems tying good absolute-continuity behavior to rectifiability and topological hypotheses.

proof_stages.label: Rough-domain hypotheses
proof_stages.summary: The admissible domains are specified by codimension-one Ahlfors regular boundary, interior corkscrews, and interior Harnack chains, excluding cusps and bad splits while retaining rough boundaries.

proof_stages.label: Existence of Robin harmonic measure
proof_stages.summary: Under the geometric hypotheses, solutions to the weak Robin problem admit a boundary representation by a family of pole-dependent Robin harmonic measures, and indicator data define set functions analogous to Dirichlet harmonic measure.

proof_stages.label: Active domains and boundary Harnack principle
proof_stages.summary: A partially reflected Brownian-motion heuristic leads to the active-domain question of Bass--Burdzy--Chen, and the speaker states that the present ADR corkscrew/Harnack-chain domains satisfy the corresponding boundary Harnack-type positivity property at the relevant small scales.

proof_stages.label: Qualitative absolute continuity without rectifiability
proof_stages.summary: The main theorem asserts that Robin harmonic measure and surface measure are mutually absolutely continuous under the rough-domain hypotheses with no rectifiability assumption, and the qualitative proof is driven by a variational characterization whose linear term vanishes on surface-null sets.

proof_stages.label: Quantitative scale-dependent absolute continuity
proof_stages.summary: A quantitative two-way smallness statement between surface measure and Robin harmonic measure is given with constants depending on the scale-invariant product of Robin parameter and radius, reflecting that rescaling changes the Robin parameter; the same variational method yields the quantitative estimates.
